senses.definition: to successfully accomplish a goal or manage to do something difficult

examples.x: The rescue team brought off the daring mission, saving all stranded climbers.
Against all odds, the students brought off a memorable school event.
Securing the gold medal, the athlete brought off an incredible performance during the championship.

senses.definition: to handle or manage something, often perceived as difficult or challenging, successfully or with confidence

examples.x: The magician's ability to carry off his tricks left the audience in awe.
Despite the technical challenges, the musician carried off the concert with flawless execution.
The chef's expertise allowed him to carry off the cooking demonstration despite unexpected ingredient substitutions.
She wasn't sure if she could carry off the lead role in the play, but her performance was outstanding.
Despite the unexpected challenges, the team managed to carry off the project on time and within budget.
The CEO's charisma and vision helped him carry off the challenging task of restructuring the company.
The diplomat's ability to navigate complex negotiations allowed him to carry off the peace talks successfully.

senses.definition: to start something with a rapid and successful beginning

examples.x: She was determined to make her singing career jump off with a memorable performance.
The team decided to jump off the project, and it quickly gained momentum.
They hope that their business will jump off to a successful start in the market.
She successfully jumped off the project with a well-received presentation.
The forces waited for the green light to jump off the attack on enemy positions.
The army was well-prepared to jump off the military campaign at dawn.

senses.definition: to cause something to begin, particularly initiating an event or process

examples.x: To kick off the project, they held a brainstorming session with the entire team.
The company plans to kick off the new marketing campaign next week.
They decided to kick off the celebration with a fireworks display.
Let's kick off the meeting with a brief overview of our goals for the project.
The teacher kicked off the semester by introducing the new curriculum.
The company kicked off the new product launch with a big advertising blitz.

senses.definition: to initiate something, especially a process, event, or discussion

examples.x: The president led off the ceremony with a speech.
The meeting will lead off with a review of the previous week's progress.
The band will lead off the concert with their most popular song.

senses.definition: to not punish someone for a wrongdoing, or only give them a light punishment
(of a plan or action) to succeed and have good results

examples.x: All those hours of studying really paid off during the exam.
Their investment in the startup paid off when the company went public.
His dedication to training paid off when he won the gold medal.
They hoped that the marketing campaign would pay off in increased sales.
Patience and perseverance often pay off in the long run.

senses.definition: to successfully achieve or accomplish something

examples.x: Despite the challenges, they managed to pull off the event.
With limited resources, he pulled off a remarkable project.
Against all odds, the team pulled off a victory.

senses.definition: to make something operate, especially by accident

examples.x: Please don't set off the car alarm while I'm inside; the keys are on the seat.
He accidentally set off the fire alarm when he burnt toast in the office kitchen.
She mistakenly set off the sprinkler system while working on the garden.

senses.definition: to formally authorize a decision, action, or document

examples.x: The committee unanimously signed off on the budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
The supervisor decided to sign off on the changes to the company policy.

senses.definition: to start an action or reaction

examples.x: His speech unintentionally sparked off a heated debate among the participants.
The news report sparked off a series of investigations into the alleged corruption.

senses.definition: to begin to act, happen, etc. in a particular manner

examples.x: The event started off with an inspiring speech from the keynote speaker.
The workshop started off with an introduction to the basic concepts of the subject.
The project will start off once the necessary approvals are obtained.
The meeting will start off with a brief introduction from the chairperson.
The book starts off with a mysterious prologue that sets the tone for the story.

senses.definition: to  make something start or happen by pushing a button, saying something, or causing a reaction

examples.x: She triggered off the alarm accidentally while trying to unlock the door.
